title: Aspirin Rectal: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text:

What side effects can this medication cause? 
What should I know about storage and disposal of this medication? 
In case of emergency/overdose 
What other information should I know? 
Other names 
Why is this medication prescribed? 
Aspirin rectal is used to reduce fever and relieve minor aches, pains and headaches. Aspirin is in a group of medications called salicylates. It works by blocking natural substances that cause fever, pain, swelling, and blood clots.
How should this medicine be used? 
Aspirin rectal comes as a suppository to insert rectally (into the rectum (the final portion of the large intestine before the anus). 
It is inserted every 4 hours as needed. Aspirin rectal is available without a prescription, but your doctor may prescribe aspirin to treat certain conditions.
Ask a doctor before you give aspirin to your teenager. Aspirin may cause Reye's syndrome (a serious condition that affects the brain and organs) in children and teenagers, especially if they have a virus like the flu or chicken pox.
Many aspirin products come in combination with other medications such as those to treat cough and cold symptoms. 
Check product labels carefully before using two or more products at the same time. 
These products may contain the same active ingredient(s) and taking them together could cause an overdose.
Stop using aspirin rectal and call your doc
